title: 1.1: Shows that Kitty was running during the analysis (e.g., process ID, debug output, or runtime logs).
1.5: Includes a stack-level or symbol-level snapshot showing the Python call stack (e.g., _run_app in kitty/main.py) or thread activity captured using inspection tools.
1.6: Includes the actual commands used for tracing or inspection (e.g., py-spy dump, strace) and shows representative raw output from those commands.
1.7: Correlates a keypress event through the input pipeline to the pty write, showing the thread that performs the actual write to the pty file descriptor (e.g., write to /dev/pts/ptmx).
1.8: Reports observing log or debug output indicating input was discarded when sent to an unfocused or closed window (e.g., 'no active window, ignoring' message).
1.9: Rules out at least two plausible but incorrect interpretations about which layer performs primary input dispatch, using timing or ordering evidence from runtime observations.
1.10: Identifies a correctness-versus-responsiveness tradeoff in input handling (e.g., mutex locking on write buffers) supported by measurable timing or ordering evidence from runtime observations.
